definition: Term premium is structurally higher from demographic-driven fiscal deterioration, but the honest confidence interval is 20-200bp, not the commonly cited 40-80bp. The input chain is fragile: confounded episodes × wide outcome range × uncertain elasticity × reserve currency attenuation. A term premium floor of 60-100bp even in deflationary scenarios is the most defensible specific claim.
evidence: Fiscal supply elasticity 20-40bp per 1pp deficit/GDP established in pre-QE regressions
Clinton 1993 (-80-100bp) and Truss 2022 (+100bp in 4 days) corroborate elasticity existence
14 confounded episodes with wide outcome range
Term premium floor logic: healthcare/pension spending rises regardless of inflation path
open_questions: Does the fiscal supply elasticity hold post-QE and for a reserve currency issuer?
What is the term premium impact of simultaneous global fiscal deterioration?
Can central bank balance sheet policies offset the demographic term premium uplift?
